title: eutassa scopulorum
intro: Eutassa scopulorum, also known as the Mountain Rosewood, is a species of plant that belongs to the family Lauraceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, particularly in the countries of Malaysia, Indonesia, and the Philippines. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Eutassa scopulorum include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating wounds, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures.
